notes.hypothesis: After wiping a single probe to an air-dry state, placing only that probe into water should create a large rapid raw ADC drop on that channel while the other dry probes remain mostly unchanged.
notes.method: s4 was wiped/air-dried and then placed into the water cup during a 90 s capture at 0.5 s cadence. The other probes remained dry/static. Timing includes manual placement time.
notes.findings: s4 dropped from 2795 to 965 raw ADC, a -1830 count response. The other channels stayed in dry ranges with small positive deltas: s1 +9, s2 +15, s3 +7 counts.
notes.conclusion: The s4 dry-to-wet response was strong, rapid, and channel-local. This supports that the water response can be isolated to one probe without obvious immediate cross-channel wetting artifacts.
